zh.lifeSummary: Élie Ier de la Flèche comte du Maine是Jean de Beaugency seigneur de La Flèche和Paula du Maine的兒子，於1060年出生在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易麥-吉洛易。他於大約1090年與Mathilde de Château-du-Loir結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1110年7月11日在法國波特-哈倫斯哈倫特享年50歲，埋葬在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易薩特勒 曼斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Flèche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Élie
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: short form of   Elisabeth .  The name was introduced into the English-speaking world (where it is often written without the accent) in the late 19th century and has become fairly popularity in recent years.

variants.name: Elisa
meanings: short form of   Elisabeth .  The name was introduced into the English-speaking world (where it is often written without the accent) in the late 19th century and has become fairly popularity in recent years.

nameMeanings.name: Ier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from Yiddish ber ‘bear’, probably influenced by the early medieval European practice of giving animal names to people. It is often paired with Dov in order to provide a Hebrew name in certain rituals.

en.lifeSummary: Élie Ier de la Flèche comte du Maine was born in 1060, in Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France as the son of Jean de Beaugency seigneur de La Flèche and Paula du Maine. He married Mathilde de Château-du-Loir about 1090. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 11 July 1110, in Charente, Poitou-Charentes, France, at the age of 50, and was buried in Le Mans, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Élie Ier de la Flèche comte du Maine est né en 1060, à Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France, fils de Jean de Beaugency seigneur de La Flèche et Paula du Maine. Il a épousé Mathilde de Château-du-Loir environ 1090. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé le 11 juillet 1110, à Charente, Poitou-Charentes, France, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à Le Mans, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, France.

it.lifeSummary: Élie Ier de la Flèche comte du Maine è nato il nel 1060 nel Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Francia come figlio di Jean de Beaugency seigneur de La Flèche e Paula du Maine. Lui sposato Mathilde de Château-du-Loir circa 1090. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì il 11 luglio 1110, nel Charente, Poitou-Charentes, Francia, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolto a Le Mans, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Francia.

ko.lifeSummary: Élie Ier de la Flèche comte du Maine(은)는 1060년 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르에서 Jean de Beaugency seigneur de La Flèche(와)과 Paula du Maine의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1090년 Mathilde de Château-du-Loir(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1110년 7월 11일 프랑스 포이토우-처츠 처트에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 사르테 일 먼스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Élie Ier de la Flèche comte du Maine nasceu em 1060, em Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, França filho de Jean de Beaugency seigneur de La Flèche e Paula du Maine. Ele casou-se com Mathilde de Château-du-Loir aproximadamente 1090. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 11 de julho de 1110, em Charente, Poitou-Charentes, França, com 50 anos, e foi sepultado em Le Mans, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Élie Ier de la Flèche comte du Maine родился в 1060 г. в местности меине-ет-лоир, пеис де ла лоир, Франция. Его родителей звали Jean de Beaugency seigneur de La Flèche и Paula du Maine. Он женился на женщине по имени Mathilde de Château-du-Loir около 1090 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 11 июля 1110 г. в местности чарент, поитоу-чарентесь, Франция в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности л манс, сарт, пеис де ла лоир, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Élie Ier de la Flèche comte du Maine nació en 1060, en Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Francia como hijo de Jean de Beaugency seigneur de La Flèche y Paula du Maine. Se casó con Mathilde de Château-du-Loir aproximadamente 1090. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 11 de julio de 1110, en Charente, Poitou-Charentes, Francia, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultado en Le Mans, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, Francia.
